text.title: feedback-management Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for feedback-management. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Lock the taxonomy in a versioned file. Treat taxonomy changes as schema migrations: write a migration script and re-tag existing items before applying.
rule.rationale: Taxonomy drift produces categorization inconsistency over time, making trend analysis unreliable. Versioning makes changes auditable and reversible.

rule.statement: Weight feedback by ARR or segment, not raw mention count. One enterprise complaint can outweigh 50 free-tier votes.
rule.rationale: Volume bias: vocal free users post many tickets; at-risk enterprise accounts may post zero. Raw count ranking optimizes for the wrong population.

rule.statement: Always store the verbatim alongside structured fields. Never lose the original quote.
rule.rationale: Agent rewrites lose intent signals. Original wording reveals tone, context, and use case that structured fields cannot capture.

rule.statement: Auto-responding without human review is a brand risk. Default mode = draft and queue; human approves before send.
rule.rationale: Wrong close-loop email (wrong feature attributed to wrong user, incorrect status) destroys trust harder than no email at all.

rule.statement: Run a quarterly drift audit: sample 50 LLM-categorized items, have a human re-tag, measure agreement rate. If below 80%, retrain or update the prompt.
rule.rationale: LLM categorization drifts as product evolves and language patterns change. Without periodic audit, the taxonomy gradually loses meaning.

rule.statement: Use embedding similarity (cosine >= 0.85) for deduplication, not full-text match. Paraphrases of the same issue are the same issue.
rule.rationale: Naive full-text dedup misses paraphrases. Counting "search is slow" and "search takes forever" as two separate requests undercounts the true signal by half.

rule.statement: Three mandatory human checkpoints: (1) before any outbound message, (2) before promoting feedback cluster to roadmap item, (3) before changing taxonomy.
rule.rationale: Each of these has irreversible effects: sent email cannot be unsent, roadmap items create expectations, taxonomy changes invalidate historical analysis.
